title: Analysis and characterization of the carbohydrate fraction of Aiptasia and coral tissue using targeted GC-MS
author: Hagen Gegner
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.s7fehjn
materials: Chemicals MOX reagent 2% methoxamine HCL in pyridine (e.g. Thermo scientific) MSTFA, 1% TMCS (10x1 ml. (e.g. Sigma 69479-10X1ML)) 4-Hydroxybenzoic acid (ISTD = Internal standard) Absolute ethanol ≥99.8% (GC) Standards for quantification ≥99.8% (GC) (This depends on the compounds targeted) We use: Glucose, Mannose, Succrose, Glycine Consumables Falcon tubes (50ml) GC sample vials with Teflon cap (2ml) Glass syringe e.g. Hamilton® syringe, 700 series Syringe filters - 0.2 micron (13 mm diameter) Beckman bottles for Beckmann Coulter centrifuge GC vial glass inlets (0.2ml) Plastic syringes (1ml) Parafilm Kim wipes (or any other tissue) Equipment GC/MS system GC (Agilent 7890A) MS (Agilent 5975C) Autosampler (Agilent 7693) Concentrator system (Labconco Centrivap Complete) Ultracentrifuge (Beckmann Coulter Avanti J-26 XP) FreezeDryer (Ultradry) Ultrasonicator (Branson digital sonifier) Homogenizer (MicroDisTec MDT 125) (if needed) Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5415 R) Thermoblock fitting for GC vials Fume hood

Preparation of: GC vials with smaller labels Heated Thermo Block for GC vials Concentrator system (max. 40 °C) Coldtrap (on) time 99=continuous (Labconco Centrivap Complete) Internal standard hydroxyl benzoic acid (ISTD) (1 ug/ul HBA in ddH2O) Standards for quantification (that depends on the targeted compounds for quantification)

Preparation of: Thermoblock for GC vials MOX (2% methoxamine HCL in pyridine) MSTFA (MSTFA, 1% TMCS) (you can also use BSTFA)) Hamilton syringes (100μl) Plastic syringes Syringe Filters Glass inserts for GC vials Teflon lids for vials MOX and MSTFA are volatile and toxic (corrosive) wear goggles, two layers of gloves and work under a fume hood. If a spill occurrs immediately switch gloves.
put samples in the thermo block for 1h at 75 °C

steps.title: At the end of your experiment
steps.instruction: At the end of your experiment: Rinse anemones or coral fragments with ddH2O to reduce the salt load Transfer anemones to cryotubes or in the case of coral fragments to a falcon tube or wrap in aluminum foil and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen Salts may interfere with the derivatization or GC measurement. Therefore, proper rinsing is advised.

steps.title: Store at -80 °C or continue...
steps.instruction: Store at -80 °C or continue with the protocol Decide on the normalization method by following the step-case below: to protein content, directly follow the rest of the protocol to dry-weight, follow the steps and then go back to step-case 'normalization to protein content' to continue with the rest of the protocol

steps.title: Transfer samples For Aiptasia simply add...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples For Aiptasia simply add 3ml ddH2O and transfer to a Falcon tube For corals use an airbrush to remove the tissue (add up to 15ml ddH2O) or mortal and pestel cooled with liquid nitrogen to grind everything to powder and add up to 15ml ddH2O If you wish to analyze the host and its symbiont separately you can add a step here to do so. Use a combination of homogenization and centrifugation to recover the symbiont cells from the host. Homogenization does not destroy Symbiodinium cells (Krediet et al. 2015). Krediet, C. J., DeNofrio, J. C., Caruso, C., Burriesci, M. S., Cella, K. and Pringle, J. R. (2015). Rapid, precise, and accurate counts of symbiodinium cells using the guava flow cytometer, and a comparison to other methods. PLoS ONE 10, 0135725. Lesser,

steps.title: Thaw samples on ice and add...
steps.instruction: Thaw samples on ice and add ddH2O For Aiptasia add 1ml ddH2O For corals use an airbrush to remove the tissue or crush/pulverize fragments in liquide nitrogen (if not done already) add up to 15ml ddH2O (the more you use the longer it takes in the next steps) At this stage you can isolate the algal symbiont from the tissue by repeated centrifugation and washing steps. See this protocol: Ochsenkühn, M. A., Röthig, T., D’Angelo, C., Wiedenmann, J. and Voolstra, C. R. (2017). The role of floridoside in osmoadaptation of coral-associated algal endosymbionts to high-salinity conditions. Sci. Adv. 3 , e1602047.

steps.title: Sonicate using a tip ultrasonicator (e
steps.instruction: Sonicate using a tip ultrasonicator (e.g. Branson digital sonifier) on ice at 7 watts for 2min until everything is dissolved. Homogenates containing symbionts will appear greenish due to the chlorophyll = symbionts are cracked open. This step depends on the sonicator and volumes used; inspection of the homogenate is advised to crack open all cells.

steps.title: Remove cell debris by centrifuging at...
steps.instruction: Remove cell debris by centrifuging at 4000g for 20min at 4 °C Keep all samples on ice.

steps.title: Move supernatant to Beckman tube (50ml)...
steps.instruction: Move supernatant to Beckman tube (50ml) and add 9 parts of absolute ethanol (cold) to 1 part of sample

steps.title: Leave them for 20min in a...
steps.instruction: Leave them for 20min in a 4 °C fridge or on ice tp precipitate DNA/RNA/Protein In the meantime balance out the Beckman tubes filled with supernatant in pairs (measure and add water when needed) Balance the tubes for the ultracentrifuge, (+-0.05mg is ok) http://null

steps.title: Centrifuge precipitate at 20
steps.instruction: Centrifuge precipitate at 20.000g for 20min at 4 °C in Beckman centrifuge The preciptant forming the pellet contains DNA, RNA and protein. Recover the pellet in ddH2O and freeze it for later total protein quantification using a BCA protein assay kit. Protein extraction from Aiptasia by Jason S Presnell

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new falcon tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new falcon tube

steps.title: Put a kim wipe on the...
steps.instruction: Put a kim wipe on the tubes lid, make holes in the lid and cool them down to -80C (optinal: use Kim wipes and parafilm) After preparation start the lyophilization (e.g. Ultradry lyophilizer) Prepare freeze dryer before taking samples from the freezer. Cool samples down (-80 C) - otherwise the falcon tubes will overflow in the lyophilizer 24:00:00, minimum lyophilization

steps.title: Suspend dried samples in 500μl of...
steps.instruction: Suspend dried samples in 500μl of ddH2O (its up to you, vials can hold 2ml) vortex vigorously

steps.title: Transfer suspended sample to a pre-labelled GC Vial
steps.instruction: Transfer suspended sample to a pre-labelled GC Vial

steps.title: Add 10μl of internal ISTD Add...
steps.instruction: Add 10μl of internal ISTD Add the internal Standard to all samples (including your calibration curves of standards for quantification)

steps.title: Dry samples using a Concentrator System (e
steps.instruction: Dry samples using a Concentrator System (e.g. Labconco Centrivap Complete)

steps.title: Store samples Keep it dry, make...
steps.instruction: Store samples Keep it dry, make sure lid is on and store in a vacuum

steps.title: Add 50μl of MOX using a...
steps.instruction: Add 50μl of MOX using a Hamilton syringe to the dried samples and close vials tightly Keep the reaction completely free of water (quenches the reagents) Use hexan or cyclohexane to clean syringes

steps.title: Remove samples from the thermo block...
steps.instruction: Remove samples from the thermo block and add 100μl of MSTFA using a Hamilton syringe Have a second syringe to break open clumps so everything is accessible for the MSTFA vortex

steps.title: Close lids tightly and leave again...
steps.instruction: Close lids tightly and leave again for 1h in the 75 °C thermo block

steps.title: After 1 h, particles can be...
steps.instruction: After 1 h, particles can be removed by syringe filtration through 0.2-micron (13 mm diameter) filters and transfer 120µL to glass inserts placed inside GC vials

steps.title: The vials are closed with PTFE...
steps.instruction: The vials are closed with PTFE septa screw caps or crimp caps and stored at -80 °C until measurement (crimp caps preferable)

steps.title: On the day of the measurement...
steps.instruction: On the day of the measurement remove samples from the freezer and let them reach room temperature before measuring. Measure in batches of ca. 10 samples and start with quality control and calibration curves first. Find the settings for the GC-MS attached in an extra document. Good luck. Agilent GC/MS system GC (Agilent 7890A) MS (Agilent 5975C) gc-ms settings.docx

steps.title: Thaw samples on ice and add...
steps.instruction: Thaw samples on ice and add ddH2O For Aiptasia add 1ml ddH2O For corals use an airbrush to remove the tissue or crush/pulverize fragments in liquide nitrogen and add up to 15ml ddH2O (the more you use the longer it takes in the next steps) At this stage you can isolate the algal symbiont from the tissue by repeated centrifugation and washing steps. See this publication for a protocol: Ochsenkühn, M. A., Röthig, T., D’Angelo, C., Wiedenmann, J. and Voolstra, C. R. (2017). The role of floridoside in osmoadaptation of coral-associated algal endosymbionts to high-salinity conditions. Sci. Adv. 3 , e1602047.

steps.title: Transfer samples to a new, pre-weighed, falcon tube
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to a new, pre-weighed, falcon tube

steps.title: Lyophilize samples over night using an type of lyophilizer (e
steps.instruction: Lyophilize samples over night using an type of lyophilizer (e.g. Ultradry) Cover the opening with a fitting pre-cut Kim Wipe piece and tighten with parafilm along the rim so the liquide can escape.

steps.title: After lyophilizing weigh the falcon tube...
steps.instruction: After lyophilizing weigh the falcon tube + dried sample for later normalization to dry-weight

steps.title: Continue with STEP-CASE
steps.instruction: Continue with STEP-CASE: Normalization to total protein content and follow the rest of the protocol.

steps.title: This protocol is a step-by-step guide...
steps.instruction: This protocol is a step-by-step guide to extract and analyze the carbohydrate fraction of Aiptasia and coral tissue. We included further notes to separate the algal symbiont fraction from the host fraction. Normalization is essential for GC-MS data, as such two options (dry-weight and total protein content) are included in this protocol. Depending on the method chosen follow the respective 'STEP-CASE' normalization to dry-weight and/or to protein content. To minimize degradation of metabolites, it is advised to work quickly and reduce waiting times. Further, it is crucial that that all steps, until the derivatization, are performed on ice/in the cold. If you are interested in other protocols related to the model organism Aiptasia: Getting started with the Aiptasia-Symbiodinium Model System by Elisha WC

steps.title: Possibility to pause the protocol and store dried samples
steps.instruction: Possibility to pause the protocol and store dried samples
